query: Angular ngOnChanges vs ngDoCheck interview question

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: Angular Lifecycle Guide
competitors.selectionReason: The Angular lifecycle guide is the canonical source for when ngOnChanges and ngDoCheck run and how often Angular checks the template.
theirStrengths: It is the official lifecycle reference and clearly states when each hook runs.
theirGaps: It is reference-first and less narrowly framed around the interview trade-off of automatic input diffs versus custom checks.

realWorldUseCases:
Change-detection boundary

ngOnChanges() is the automatic hook for @Input() reference changes, while ngDoCheck() is the escape hatch for custom change detection when Angular's default checks are not enough. The production difference is cost and precision: ngOnChanges tells you which inputs changed, but ngDoCheck runs frequently and should stay rare.
followUpConfusion:
Rule of thumb
  • Prefer ngOnChanges for normal input-driven logic.
  • Reach for ngDoCheck only when you can explain the mutation or diff Angular will not catch.
  • Treat ngDoCheck as a cost center because it runs on every detection cycle.

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.
followUpConfusion: Our updated page now answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly with a sharper rule, boundary, or failure pattern.

actionableExamples: Keep one compact worked example near the top so the answer stays faster to scan than a broader reference.

competitors.winReason: The updated page now wins on production framing and follow-up confusion while the competitor remains competitive on raw examples.
